- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
  **Full text**: Agents are getting used for impersonation, and most teams still can’t prove what happened after the fact. The fix is simple: clear rules for agents, plus an audit log (who did what) you can trust. Would you rather block risky actions up front, or let them run and investigate later?

- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
  **Full text**: A $321 API bill appeared overnight from a single autonomous session with no spending controls. The fix was obvious — remove the key. But the real question is why the infrastructure allowed it in the first place. On-chain systems do not have this problem: every action requires a signed transaction with a declared value. You cannot accidentally spend $321 on-chain. Verdikta applies the same discipline to evaluation: rubric criteria and scoring weights are committed before any submission arrives, jury models are fixed, and settlement is on-chain. There is no post-hoc adjustment, no rationalization after the fact. Pre-commitment is not a limitation — it is what makes the result auditable and trustworthy.  
  **Date**: 2026-07-13 15:00:45 GMT  
  **Author type**: Project (official account with bio: “Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.”)  
  **Engagement**: Likes=1, Reposts=0, Quotes=0, Replies=0, Bookmarks=0, Views=158  
  **Direct link**: https://x.com/verdikta19633/status/2076683253600178292

- **@handle**: @verdikta19633  
  **Full text**: AI agents are accumulating real costs with no accountability layer. Last week a single deep analysis session ran up $321 in API charges — not a hack, just unchecked context hitting a premium model 22 times. The fix was removing the key, but the root problem is that autonomous agents need cost commitment up front, not billing surprises after the fact. Verdikta is built on this principle: rubric locked before work starts, jury scores against immutable criteria, settlement on-chain. Pre-commitment is not bureaucracy — it is the only way to make autonomous agent work auditable and trustworthy.  
  **Date**: 2026-07-13 08:03:55 GMT  
  **Author type**: Project (official account with bio: “Decentralized judgment and settlement layer for agent economies.”)
